Title: Proposed KWAN laws widen funding base beyond Petronas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The newly-passed National Trust Fund (KWAN) bill is expected to reduce the fund's reliance on Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) by introducing new statutory funding sources while allowing contributions from a wider range of donors.
Petronas has contributed a total of RM13.5 billion to the KWAN since its establishment nearly four decades ago.
Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the proposed legislation marks a shift from the existing framework, under which Petronas has remained KWAN's sole voluntary contributor since its establishment.
Liew said the bill would, for the first time, introduce three mandatory statutory funding streams while retaining provisions for voluntary contributions from state governments, grants, donations, endowments, wills and entities involved in the exploration or development of depleting natural resources.
"The fund will no longer depend on the discretion of a single contributor. The new framework broadens the sources of contributions while ensuring a more sustainable flow of funds," he said while winding up debate on the second reading of the National Trust Fund Bill in the Dewan Rakyat.
KWAN's assets stood at RM22.43 billion as at the end of 2024, with Petronas continuing to make voluntary contributions since the fund was established in 1988.
Liew said the revamped framework is expected to strengthen KWAN's role as an intergenerational savings fund, with its net assets projected to double in about 10 years and continue doubling every decade through consistent statutory contributions and compounded investment returns.
Addressing concerns over the impact on Petronas, he said the bill would not interfere with the national oil company's discretion in determining the amount of dividends paid to the government.
Instead, he noted that the proposed law requires that at least two per cent of any dividends received by the government from Petronas be channelled into KWAN.
"The contribution comes from the government's share of the dividend and is not an additional levy imposed on Petronas," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan polls: Media centres to open for election coverage from tomorrow
Article:
SEREMBAN: Three media centres will be opened for the Negri Sembilan election from tomorrow until Aug 1 by the Communications Ministry through the Information Department and the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).
In a statement, the ministry said the facilities will be located at Hotel Seri Malaysia in Seremban, the Kampung Paya National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) in Port Dickson, and the Kampung Gentam Nadi in Kuala Pilah.
"Media practitioners may use the facilities and access information available at the media centres, while those reporting from other areas can utilise facilities and internet access at 60 Nadi centres throughout Negri Sembilan designated as support media centres," it said.
The ministry said MCMC will also set up complaint counters at the media centres to monitor and coordinate issues involving internet connectivity, telecommunications disruptions, and online content related to religion, race and the royal institution (3R), as well as scams and impersonation.
The locations of NADI centres are available at https://www.nadi.my/lokasi-nadi.
The Election Commission has fixed Saturday (July 18) as nomination day for the Negri Sembilan election, with early voting on July 28 and polling on Aug 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Russia and Ukraine strike vessels in Black Sea, wheat prices jump
Article:
MOSCOW: Ukraine and Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Thursday on vessels in the Black Sea and the Sea of Azov, stepping up hostilities in a zone vital for grain exports that have triggered a rise in global wheat prices.
Ukraine's military said it struck six Russian tankers and two tugboats, while Russia's defence ministry said its forces hit a maritime vessel and a speedboat of the Ukrainian armed forces while they were en route to ports in the Odesa region.
Russian forces also struck a number of other military and industrial targets, the ministry said. Ukraine has stepped up wider attacks on Russian energy targets, including oil refineries and tankers, seeking to undermine Moscow's war effort.
Russia also has intensified strikes on Ukraine's deepwater Black Sea ports in the Greater Odesa area, which handle much of the country's grain and other cargo and are vital to its wartime economy.
The Ukrainian attacks have forced Russia, the world's top grain exporter, to limit shipping in the Sea of Azov — a route that handles about a quarter of its grain exports, sources told Reuters. Shipping remained restricted on Thursday, they said.
"Navigation both to and from the sea is not being carried out for now," a source said.
Two of Ukraine's three Black Sea ports wereoperating normallyon Thursday morning while the port of Chornomorsk had sharply reduced its grain intake, the Ukrainian state railway Ukrzaliznytsia said.
It said 901,300 metric tons of grain had been sent toward the ports since the start of July, "which is, of course, less than last month." Ukrainian officials said Russian ballistic missiles struck at least two districts in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, early on Thursday, triggering fires and killing two people.
Authorities in at least three Russian regions also reported deaths and injuries inflicted by Ukrainian drone and rocket strikes overnight.
On Wednesday, European wheat prices jumped seven per cent as the escalating attacks in the Black Sea raised concerns over key grain export routes, with traders expecting some demand to shift to European Union supplies.
Benchmark September milling wheat on Paris-based Euronext ended the daytime trading session seven per cent higher at €231.75 (US$265) per metric tonne, a price not seen since February 2025.
Chicago Board of Trade wheat futures surged five per cent on Wednesday , though they pared their gains in early Thursday morning trade.
Ukraine has lost about a third of its capacity to export grain via its Black Sea ports due to the intensifying Russian missile and drone attacks, the country's main farmers' union said.
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha called for restoration of the freedom of navigation in the Black Sea.
"This is also a fundamental issue for Ukraine. It is vital, as the Black Sea is the main route for the export of Ukrainian goods," he told reporters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saarani: Perak unity govt to remain intact until end of term
Article:
IPOH: The Perak unity government will remain intact until the end of its term despite recent political developments in Melaka.
Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said all state executive council (exco) members had reaffirmed their commitment to continue working together until the dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly.
He said the coalition, comprising BN and PH, remained stable and was unaffected by DAP's decision to withdraw its support for the Melaka state government.
"After DAP announced its withdrawal from the Melaka unity government, I met all my exco members yesterday before the state executive council meeting.
"I asked them frankly whether what happened in Melaka would happen in Perak. As of yesterday, the answer was no. We have pledged to continue serving the people because we still have a long way to go.
"Whatever happens after the State Legislative Assembly is dissolved is another matter, but until then, we will continue to serve as usual.
"There is no issue because we can still work together," he said.
Saarani, who is also Perak BN chairman, told reporters after opening the Halaman Suri Sejahtera Negeri Perak at Jalan Jumbo Jet in Taman Lapangan Terbang here today.
Also present was Perak Women, Family, Social Welfare, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman Datuk Salbiah Mohamed.
The Halaman Suri Sejahtera was established as a safe, comfortable and accessible community hub where programmes on economic empowerment, family development and community engagement will be held.
On Tuesday, Melaka DAP announced its immediate withdrawal from the state government after the Melaka State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the state constitution to allow the appointment of nominated assemblymen.
The party said the move was consistent with its opposition to the
amendments
, which it viewed as being inconsistent with democratic principles and the electoral process.
Saarani also echoed former prime minister Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad's oft-quoted remark that politics has no permanent friends or enemies.
He said political differences should not lead to personal attacks or damaged relationships.
"We should not condemn, insult or abuse one another, or sever ties simply because we have differing views. We must also be careful not to say things that hurt or offend others.
"I do not quite agree with the use of the word 'conflict'. It is more appropriate to describe it as differences in opinion and approach," he said.
The state executive council comprises four BN representatives, including the
menteri besar, four from DAP, two from PKR and one from Amanah.
The state government commands the support of 33 of the state's 59 assemblymen, comprising 24 from PH, including 18 from DAP, and nine from BN.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese banks set for US$41mil payday from chipmaker's US$8.6bil IPO
Article:
SINGAPORE/HONG KONG: Six Chinese investment banks working on chipmaker CXMT's US$8.6 billion IPO are set to take home at least US$41 million in fees, company filings showed, in a boost for an industry whose income pool has shrunk over the past half decade.
The payout would bring the year's total fees from mainland IPOs to about US$684.62 million, gaining on last year's US$984.75 million, LSEG data showed. That compared with a 2022 peak of US$4.16 billion on IPOs from heavyweights including China Mobile and CNOOC.
The share sale continues a revival of the onshore IPO market as the government lowers barriers for AI, semiconductor and robotics companies aiming to raise money from public markets, in a push for technological advancement.
While CXMT's fee rate of 0.48 per cent of IPO proceeds is well below market average, the figure indicates its bargaining power in one of China's most high-profile listings of the year.
ChangXin Memory Technologies (CXMT) opened its IPO for subscription on Thursday. The firm is China's biggest maker of dynamic random-access memory (DRAM) chips that are used in smartphones, computers, servers and other electronics.
If CXMT raises US$8.6 billion, the initial public offering will be Asia's biggest so far this year as well as the biggest-ever Chinese A-share semiconductor IPO, surpassing that of Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp (SMIC) in 2020.
China Securities and CICC are sponsoring the IPO, or playing lead roles in the share sale, IPO filings showed.
Other banks involved are China Merchants Securities , Guotai Haitong Securities, Guoyuan Securities and Huatai Securities unit Huatai United Securities, the filings showed.
CXMT expects fees of 280.6 million yuan, equal to about 0.48 per cent of total proceeds. That compares with the 4.52 per cent average for China A-share IPOs this year, LSEG data showed.
If demand triggers an overallotment option, proceeds could reach US$9.8 billion with fees at 296 million yuan, the filings showed.
"This high-profile IPO carries both commercial value and strategic importance," said Shen Meng, director at boutique bank Chanson & Co in Beijing.
Competition to take part in the IPO pushes down fees, Shen said. "Nevertheless, given CXMT's huge fundraising size, investment banks can still reap substantial proceeds even with relatively reduced fee rates."
CICC declined to comment. CXMT, China Securities, China Merchants Securities, Guotai Haitong Securities, Guoyuan Securities and Huatai United Securities did not respond to requests for comment.
China Securities, CICC, Guotai Haitong and Huatai Securities ranked among the top five China A-share IPO fee earners from 2022 to 2026 year-to-date, LSEG data showed. China Merchants was ninth.
The CXMT fee is also small relative to other recent deals. Last month, China Resources New Energy paid 0.65 per cent for its 24.5 billion yuan Shenzhen listing.
In the US, SpaceX paid US$500 million for its record US$75 billion IPO last month, equal to about 0.67 per cent of proceeds, while South Korea's SK Hynix paid about 0.97 per cent of US$26.5 billion raised via American depositary receipts last week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two Hong Kong drug arrests raise concerns over recruitment of Malaysian drug mules
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two Malaysians have been arrested in separate drug trafficking cases at Hong Kong International Airport within three days, as authorities believe international syndicates continue recruiting drug mules from Malaysia.
The first arrest took place on July 7, when Hong Kong Customs seized about 1kg of suspected cocaine worth an estimated HK$800,000 (RM416,000) from a 21-year-old Malaysian man who arrived on a flight from Kuala Lumpur.
Hong Kong Customs said officers discovered the suspected cocaine concealed inside the shoes worn by the passenger during customs clearance.
He was arrested and subsequently charged with one count of trafficking in a dangerous drug before being produced at the West Kowloon magistrate's court on July 8.
On July 9, another Malaysian,
a 19-year-old from Sarawak, was arrested after Hong Kong Customs allegedly found 3kg of cannabis buds
worth about HK$500,000 (RM260,000) in his checked baggage.
Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan confirmed the latest arrest, saying Hong Kong authorities had informed Malaysian police of the case.
"Yes, the arrest has been confirmed by the Hong Kong authorities. Hong Kong Customs found 3kg of cannabis buds.
"The suspect is a 19-year-old Malaysian from Sarawak with no previous criminal record in Malaysia. He is believed to have been recruited by a drug syndicate to act as a drug mule," Hussein said.
Hong Kong Customs said it would continue intensifying enforcement against transnational drug trafficking through intelligence-led operations and risk-based passenger screening, particularly involving travellers arriving from high-risk regions.
Under Hong Kong's Dangerous Drugs Ordinance, drug trafficking carries a maximum penalty of a HK$5 million (RM2.6 million) fine and life imprisonment upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher on broad-based buying
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index rebounded from yesterday's losses to close higher on Thursday, driven by broad-based buying across multiple sectors.
At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 8.43 points, or 0.49 per cent, to close at 1,722.19 from Wednesday's close of 1,713.76.
The benchmark index opened 3.0 points higher at 1,716.76, marking its intraday low, and hit a high of 1,726.75 during the midday session.
On the broader market, gainers led losers 576 to 550, while 563 counters were unchanged, 1,064 untraded, and 12 suspended.
Turnover declined to 3.68 billion units valued at RM2.80 billion from 4.07 billion units valued at RM2.84 billion on Wednesday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of Equity Research Thong Pak Leng remains cautiously optimistic as the gradual return of foreign fund inflows continues to provide support to the local market.
"As such, we expect the FBM KLCI to maintain its positive momentum and gradually advance towards the 1,720-1,730 range by the end of the week, supported by selective buying interest in fundamentally strong large-cap stocks, particularly within the financial, plantation and selected industrial sectors," he told Bernama.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said investor sentiment was underpinned by expectations that Malaysia's second-quarter gross domestic product (GDP), scheduled for release tomorrow, will remain above the four per cent mark, reinforcing confidence in the country's resilient economic expansion.
He said optimism was further supported by Wall Street's overnight gains after softer US wholesale inflation and another round of strong bank earnings strengthened expectations that the Federal Reserve could begin easing monetary policy later this year.
Among heavyweights, Maybank jumped eight sen to RM11.04 and Public Bank rose 15 sen to RM5.20. Tenaga Nasional fell eight sen to RM14.30, CIMB was one sen weaker at RM7.70, and IHH Healthcare slipped nine sen to RM8.53.
As for the active stocks, Tanco added half a sen to 32 sen, Nationgate soared 20 sen to RM1.17, VS Industry perked up 1.5 sen to 23.5 sen, SFP Tech rose four sen to 34 sen, while HHRG slid two sen to 14.5 sen.
Of the top gainers, Nestle went up RM1.00 to RM93.00, United Plantations climbed 30 sen to RM35.00, Critical Holdings surged 24 sen to RM1.90, and Fraser & Neave put on 18 sen to RM29.28.
For top losers, Malaysian Pacific Industries dipped 46 sen to RM45.28, UMS Integration decreased 28 sen to RM8.03, Hong Leong Industries shed 18 sen to RM18.42, and Kuala Lumpur Kepong lost 14 sen to RM21.66.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCMC orders harmful online content blocked within 24 hours
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Communications Ministry, through the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), has made it mandatory for licensed service providers to block high-priority harmful online content within 24 hours.
The measure, enforced through the Online Safety (Period) Regulations 2025 under the Online Safety Act 2025 (Act 866), aims to curb the spread of fake news, disinformation, and misinformation at an early stage.
"For high-priority content, such as financial fraud, the content must be made inaccessible within 24 hours to enable assessment, verification, and further action to be taken," the ministry said in a written reply in the Dewan Rakyat today.
The ministry was responding to a question from Rodziah Ismail (PH-Ampang) on the mechanism for curbing the spread of fake news, disinformation, and misinformation before such content becomes widely disseminated.
According to the ministry, from Jan 1, 2022, to May 31, 2026, MCMC submitted 63,215 requests to service providers to remove false content, resulting in the successful takedown of 54,626 pieces of content, or 86 per cent.
In addition to enforcing Act 866 and the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, MCMC utilised artificial intelligence (AI) technology through the launch of the Artificial Intelligence Fact-check Assistant (AIFA) Chatbot on Jan 28, 2025, to help the public verify information.
As of May 31, AIFA, which supports the Sebenarnya.my portal, had processed 197,403 messages, averaging 404 messages per day, with 1,016 fact-check articles published between Jan 1, 2022, and May 31, 2026.
To strengthen digital literacy and preventive measures, the Safe Internet Campaign has been implemented in schools and higher learning institutions, alongside 1,938 public programmes involving 2,136,941 participants nationwide as of June 15.
"By combining law enforcement measures, strategic collaboration with platform providers, technology utilisation, and digital literacy empowerment, the government is committed to addressing the spread of fake news, disinformation, and misinformation more effectively to protect the security and well-being of the country's digital ecosystem," the ministry said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Network School probe: No evidence yet of Israelis in programme, says Immigration
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department has yet to find evidence supporting claims that Israelis are participating in the Network School programme in Forest City, Johor.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said investigations into the allegations were ongoing, but no substantial evidence had been found so far.
He said several government agencies were also conducting investigations within their respective jurisdictions.
"We are still investigating the allegations that have been made. Investigations are being carried out by various agencies according to their respective scopes and roles," he told the New Straits Times today.
Zakaria said the department would continue its investigations and share its findings with the relevant agencies.
"From the Immigration Department's side, we will continue our investigations and share the findings with the relevant agencies.
"So far, we have not found any evidence to verify the allegations regarding the presence of Israelis in the Network School programme," he said.
The issue gained attention after Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said on Tuesday that any Israeli citizens found to be in Malaysia following investigations would be
ordered to leave
the country.
Anwar also reiterated that Malaysia did not recognise Israeli passports and that anyone found to have entered the country in breach of immigration laws would face appropriate action.
Yesterday, Zakaria said Immigration officers inspected an international community in Forest City, where 266 foreigners from 40 countries were screened.
The operation was conducted with the Iskandar Puteri City Council (MBIP), Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), Johor State Secretary's Office and the police.
"Preliminary checks found that all those inspected possessed valid immigration documents based on current records."
However, he said further investigations were continuing to ensure compliance with entry requirements, the Immigration Act 1959/63 and other relevant laws.
He added that further checks would be carried out if new information emerged regarding identity fraud, travel documents, immigration facilities or breaches of pass conditions.
The matter has drawn national attention following allegations that Israelis had entered Malaysia using passports from countries where they also held citizenship to participate in the Network School programme at Forest City.
The Johor government has called for a
comprehensive investigation
into the identities and nationalities of those involved, travel documents, use of second passports, passes issued and the purpose of their entry into Malaysia. It also sought a review of the programme's operations, including licences, premises, land conditions, accommodation arrangements and business activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH ready for any BN-PN election strategy in Negri Sembilan, says Loke
Article:
SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan is prepared for any political strategy by its rivals, including possible cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, ahead of the Negri Sembilan state election.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said that, based on previous political developments, including the Johor election, PH would not be unsettled by any political manoeuvres by its opponents and would instead remain focused on strengthening its own election machinery.
He said PH's priority at present is to reinforce unity among its component parties to secure victory in the contested seats.
"We have already anticipated this, and it is clear. That is their choice.
"PH will focus on our own strengths to win the seats we are contesting. What is important is the strength and unity among PH's component parties in facing the election. Whatever the challenges and competition, ultimately it is up to the people to decide," he told reporters today.
Loke spoke after officiating the 2026 Kampun Angkat Madani, Sekolah Angkat Madani and Santuni Madani programmes organised by the Transport Ministry in Kampung Mantin Dalam near here.
He was responding to a question on cooperation between BN and PAS over their seat allocation strategy and constituency negotiations for the state polls.
Commenting on the expected keen contest, including claims that Chinese voter support could shift to opposition parties such as MCA, Loke said it was ultimately up to the people to decide.
"We will also put up a strong fight. I don't think there is any need to comment too much because, before an election, every party can claim it has public support. What matters is the outcome on polling day," he said.
Loke, who is the incumbent Chennah assemblyman, said the performance of the Negri Sembilan government under the leadership of Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun since 2018 would be PH's main asset in continuing to earn the people's confidence.
Meanwhile, commenting on the political impasse in Melaka following Melaka DAP's decision to withdraw from the state administration over the appointment of nominated assembly members, he said the decision was final.
"We stated our position yesterday and respect the decision made by Melaka DAP. The state government has also rearranged the seating in the State Legislative Assembly, so the matter is now settled and final," he said.
Loke also dismissed claims that the Madanni Adopted Village initiative to deliver assistance and improve rural infrastructure was a seasonal effort linked to the upcoming election.
He said the programme had been implemented continuously by every ministry since 2025 to ensure basic facilities in villages continue to be upgraded.
Kampung Baru Mantin was selected under the Madani Adopted Village initiative, while Kampung Mantin Dalam was chosen under the Santuni Madani programme. SJKC Chung Hua Mantin was selected as a Madani Adopted School.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court of Appeal upholds 'Mentega Terbang' film ban
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal today upheld the government's prohibition on the screening, distribution, and promotion of the film "Mentega Terbang".
A three-member bench comprising Justices Datuk Ismail Brahim, Datuk Ong Chee Kwan, and Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh unanimously dismissed an appeal by the film's director, Mohd Khairianwar Jailani, and producer, Tan Meng Kheng.
The ruling affirms the High Court's decision to reject their judicial review application.
Delivering the unanimous decision, Justice Ong said the home minister had lawfully exercised his powers under Section 26 of the Film Censorship Act 2002 (FCA) in issuing the prohibition order.
"The order is not tainted by illegality or irrationality," he said.
Justice Ong said there was sufficient objective material to support the minister's decision, including the Film Censorship Board's evaluation report, an assessment by the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim), the Special Branch's opinion, numerous police reports, and the home minister's own assessment after viewing the film.
He also ruled that Section 26 of the FCA, which empowers the home minister to prohibit the exhibition, distribution, or possession of a film in the public interest, is constitutional.
The court added that Khairianwar and Tan were not entitled to be heard before the prohibition order was issued.
On Aug 21, 2023, the home minister issued the order under Section 26 of the FCA prohibiting the exhibition, display, distribution, possession, circulation, and sale of the film.
In December 2023, Khairianwar and Tan filed a judicial review application against the home minister and the government to quash the prohibition order.
They said the decision to ban the film was irrational, violated the constitutional right to freedom of expression, and caused them financial losses because the film could not be screened or distributed anywhere in the country.
The applicants also sought a certiorari order to quash the ban and a declaration that Section 26 of the FCA is ultra vires Article 10 (1) and (2) and Article 8 (1) of the Federal Constitution.
The High Court dismissed their judicial review on Jan 6 last year, leading to the present appeal.
The film, broadcast on a video streaming platform, sparked public backlash and anger for allegedly portraying scenes that involve conflicting religious beliefs and elements.
During the appeal hearing, lawyer N. Surendran, representing Khairianwar and Tan, said Section 26 of the FCA infringed the right to freedom of speech guaranteed under Article 10 of the Federal Constitution.
Senior Federal Counsel Mohammad Sallehuddin Md Ali, appearing for the respondents, said the film contained content that insulted Islam and touched on religious sensitivities, adding that allowing it to continue being screened could trigger unrest and anger within the Muslim community.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia is fifth largest source of foreign investment in Indonesia's Q2
Article:
JAKARTA: Malaysia was the fifth-largest source of foreign investment in Indonesia in the second quarter (Q2) of 2026, contributing US$700 million, while Indonesia's realised investment totalled Rp511.8 trillion.
Investment and Downstreaming Minister Rosan Roeslani said Hong Kong was its largest source of foreign investment in Q2 at US$5.5 billion, followed by Singapore (US$4.2 billion), China (US$1.7 billion) and Japan (US$900 million).
He said the rankings reflect the jurisdictions through which investments entered Indonesia, noting that funds recorded from Malaysia, Singapore and Hong Kong may not necessarily originate from companies based in those economies.
"The investment is recorded based on the country through which it enters Indonesia, so it does not necessarily originate from that country," he told a press conference, streamed live on YouTube, on Thursday.
Rosan said investment realisation in Q2 rose 7.1 per cent year-on-year (y-o-y) to Rp511.8 trillion, creating 742,223 jobs, up 5.1 per cent from the same period last year.
Total investment realised in the first half (H1) of 2026 was Rp1,010.6 trillion, up 7.2 per cent y-o-y, representing 49.5 per cent of its annual target. It generated employment for 1,448,862 people, a 15 per cent rise from a year earlier, he said.
Singapore remained as Indonesia's largest foreign investor in H1 2026, with total realised investment of US$8.8 billion, followed by Hong Kong (US$7.8 billion), China (US$3.9 billion), Japan (US$1.9 billion) and the United States (US$1.7 billion), he said.
Rosan said the government continues to improve the investment climate through regulatory reforms, including Government Regulation No 28 introduced last October, which he said had provided greater certainty in the licensing process. This was well received by investors.
"Our neighbouring countries are also reforming their policies and regulations, so Indonesia must continue to do the same," he said.
Investment in downstream industries reached Rp152.7 trillion in Q2, accounting for 29.8 per cent of total realised investment, up 5.7 per cent year-on-year.
Rosan said bauxite overtook nickel as the largest downstream investment commodity in Q2, driven by several new projects undertaken by domestic and foreign investors.
He said the government will continue expanding downstream development beyond minerals to include palm oil, rubber, timber, silica sand and other commodities identified in Indonesia's downstream blueprint.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UST Malaysia chief recognised with Penang state award
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: UST Malaysia vice president and country head Amar Chhajer has been awarded the Darjah Johan Negeri (DJN) by Penang Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Ramli Ngah Talib.
This was in recognition of Chhajer's contributions to the state's technology and business ecosystem.
The award was presented during the Penang state investiture ceremony in George Town.
This marks Chhajer's second state recognition from Penang, following the Pingat Jasa Kebaktian awarded to him in 2020.
Chhajer, who serves as vice president and country head of UST Malaysia, oversees the company's business growth across Southeast Asia.
"It is an honour to receive the Darjah Johan Negeri. This recognition is a testament to UST's mission of transforming lives through technology, and it belongs to our team in Malaysia, who bring that mission to life every day for our clients and communities.
"Penang has been home to me for many years, and to be recognised by the state I have worked alongside is deeply humbling," he said.
Under Chhajer's leadership, UST Malaysia has expanded its presence in digital transformation, artificial intelligence (AI), silicon engineering and product engineering services.
Beyond his corporate role, Chhajer serves as an adviser to the Penang Silicon Design @5km initiative, which aims to strengthen the state's position as a regional semiconductor design hub.
Chhajer has also held leadership roles within the Global Business Services (GBS) Focus Group Penang, including as chairman and deputy chairman, and currently serves as an adviser to the group.
In addition, he is a committee member of the GBS Council Malaysia and a TalentCorp TF50 Fellow, contributing to talent development and industry growth initiatives.
The company said the recognition also reflects UST Malaysia's broader commitment to supporting economic development, talent growth and community engagement in Penang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sara hits 99pct utilisation rate, RM3.45 billion spent
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (Sara) programme recorded a 99 per cent utilisation rate among nine million monthly Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) recipients, with spending reaching RM3.45 billion this year.
The Finance Ministry said under the Sara Untuk Semua initiative, some 22 million people, or 87 per cent of all recipients, generated over RM1.77 billion in local market transactions.
"Recipient spending performance is a key gauge of how effectively STR and Sara are easing cost-of-living pressures for low- and middle-income groups.
"It also boosts the local economy through multiplier effects," the ministry said in a written reply posted on the Parliament website today.
The reply was in response to Datuk Aminolhuda Hassan (PH-Sri Gading), who asked about the government's key performance indicators for the two programmes.
Sara is a cashless aid scheme delivered via MyKad credits, redeemable at registered Sara Rakan Niaga outlets for 15 essential categories, ranging from basic food and personal care items to household cleaning supplies and medicines.
"This enables the government to track the programme's progress effectively and make sure aid reaches the intended recipients for its stated purposes," the ministry said.
It added that STR and Sara allocations have risen to RM15 billion in 2026, from RM10 billion in 2024, reflecting the government's push for a stronger, more targeted social safety net.
The ministry pledged to continue monitoring the aid programmes to ensure support flows to those who need it most amid rising living costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: All 16 state education departments to host MSSM events this year
Article:
BANGI: All 16 state education departments, including those in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan, will host Malaysian Schools Sports Council (MSSM) events for the first time this year.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the milestone was made possible through close collaboration between the Education Ministry, state education departments and district education offices, allowing wider participation in school sports nationwide.
"Earlier, I asked (Education Ministry Sports, Co-curriculum and Arts Division director) Cikgu Ithnin what was special about this year's MSSM.
"He told me that, in the past, financial and cost constraints meant we were unable to organise events in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan every year.
"Instead, hosting duties were rotated, and Labuan, in particular, was sometimes given fewer opportunities.
"But for the first time this year, MSSM has involved all 16 state education departments, covering Peninsular Malaysia, the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan," he said.
He was speaking at the Education Ministry-MSSM-MILO Sports Awards 2025 at Tenera Hotel & Suites here today.
Wong said many outstanding MSSM athletes had gone on to become key figures in Malaysia's sporting landscape.
"Our talented athletes do not stop at MSSM.
"Many of those we celebrate today will go on to become the backbone of Malaysia's sporting future," he said.
Meanwhile, Wong said the KPM-MSSM-MILO Sports Awards had produced more than 500 high-performance athletes since their introduction in 1968.
"Who does not know Datuk Mohd Azizulhasni Awang, our national track cycling legend, or Datuk Pandelela Rinong, Malaysia's diving queen?
"These sporting icons are among the best examples of athletes whose talents were identified and nurtured through this 'Pentas Juara' from their school days.
"We are equally proud of national sprint star Muhammad Azeem Fahmi, who recently graduated from Auburn University in the United States," he said.
Wong said the ministry remained committed to strengthening school sports to ensure students were given opportunities to develop their talents and realise their potential at the highest level.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Asian LNG prices surge to highest since late March amid Hormuz supply disruption
Article:
ISTANBUL: Asian liquefied natural gas (LNG) prices climbed to their highest level since late March as renewed hostilities around the Strait of Hormuz disrupted supplies from the Persian Gulf and prompted buyers to seek replacement cargoes, Anadolu Ajansi reported.
The Japan-Korea Marker, the benchmark for spot LNG deliveries to Northeast Asia, rose 19.66 per cent on Wednesday to US$19.93 per million British thermal units.
The benchmark has gained 25.03 per cent over the past month and was 60.66 per cent higher than a year earlier.
Fresh hostilities in and around the Strait of Hormuz have restricted LNG shipments from the Persian Gulf, which accounts for roughly 20 per cent of global supply.
The disruption has particularly affected Qatari exports, which must pass through the strategic waterway to reach international markets.
Asian importers, including Pakistan and Bangladesh, are scrambling to replace lost Qatari shipments, increasing competition for available spot cargoes and placing further upward pressure on prices.
The Strait of Hormuz connects the Persian Gulf with the Gulf of Oman and the Arabian Sea and is a critical route for global oil and LNG trade.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Traffic cop jailed four months, fined RM1,000 for bribery
Article:
KUANTAN: A traffic police corporal was sentenced to four months' jail by the Sessions Court here today after being found guilty on four counts of accepting bribes amounting to RM1,000.
Judge Sazlina Safie made the decision after finding that the defence had failed to raise reasonable doubt against the case faced by Ahmad Amin Khairul Komar, 42, at the end of the defence case.
"After examining the entirety of the evidence from both the prosecution and the defence, the court decides that at the end of this defence case, the prosecution has successfully proven the case against the accused person beyond reasonable doubt.
"The prosecution has successfully proven all elements of the offences for the four amended charges under Section 165 of the Penal Code, and the accused failed to rebut the presumption under Section 50(3) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act," she said.
According to the charge sheet, Ahmad Amin was charged with four counts of receiving RM1,000 via online money transfers into his wife's account from an individual between Oct 3, 2017, and March 2, 2018.
The court sentenced him to four months' imprisonment for each charge and ordered all sentences to run concurrently starting today.
However, Sazlina allowed an application by defence lawyer Melissa Sukri to stay the execution of the sentence pending an appeal at the High Court, despite opposition from the prosecution.
The court granted the accused bail of RM10,000 with one surety.
MACC deputy public prosecutor Ezuain Farhana Ahmad handled the prosecution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Psychiatrist highlights complexity of suicide risk factors in Zara inquest
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A child's perception of a positive environment and interactions is important when assessing suicide risk factors, rather than focusing solely on parental separation and conflict, a psychiatrist told the coroner's court.
Consultant psychiatrist Dr Wong Haw Huo told the court that clinicians had seen many cases involving children whose parents were not separated but who were still associated with suicidal behaviour.
"In reality, as clinicians, we see many patients whose parents were not separated, yet their children were still suicidal.
"I would say suicidology is a very complex medical phenomenon. There is still ongoing research, and it is based on probability," he told coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 82nd day of the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.
He added that children would generally prefer to have both parents together, provided they were able to create a loving and nurturing environment.
The 39-year-old expert witness was responding to conducting officer Mohd Fairuz Johari's question on whether parental separation and conflict are recognised as suicide risk factors.
A year ago today, the 13-year-old was found unconscious on the ground floor of the SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha hostel. She died the following day.
During the interview, Dr Wong testified that Zara's father, Mahathir Hashim, had described his marriage to Noraidah Lamat as brief and "rushed".
"He (Mahathir) attributed the breakdown of the marriage primarily to Noraidah.
"He described her as unwilling to care for his children and alleged infidelity. He denied any wrongdoing on his part," Dr Wong said, adding that Mahathir had also reported that his three children from another marriage had previously stayed with Noraidah.
Despite having a distant relationship with her father and limited communication with him, Dr Wong said Zara had protected Mahathir's reputation.
He said when someone asked about her father's whereabouts, Zara would say Mahathir was working.
The inquest will resume tomorrow.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor to produce AI cleaning robots under China partnership
Article:
KULAI: Johor is set to become the production base for artificial intelligence (AI)-powered cleaning robots under a technology transfer partnership between a local company and a Chinese robotics firm.
Indah Holding Venture (M) Sdn Bhd director Datuk B Ravintharan said the company plans to manufacture the robots at its Senai factory for export and the domestic market.
Ravintharan said the collaboration followed an introduction by the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) to a Chinese robotics company seeking a local partner.
He said Indah Holding, which currently provides conventional cleaning services, has invested about RM850,000 in its factory, renovations and production facilities.
The first robot, valued at about RM35,000, is expected to arrive on July 26 or 27 for testing, assembly and production preparation.
"Our first customer has projected demand of up to 70,000 units a year. Based on that forecast, we are targeting annual sales of between RM18 million and RM20 million during the initial phase from 2026 to 2028.
"Within five years, we aim to increase annual sales to RM50 million by expanding into new markets and lowering production costs," he told Bernama.
Ravintharan said the robots will be manufactured in Johor before being exported to the United Arab Emirates, the United States and Europe. The company also plans to introduce them to the domestic market within one to two years, targeting commercial users such as shopping malls, factories, airports and data centres.
He said the project is expected to create hundreds of jobs in its initial phase, particularly for local technicians and engineers.
"We want to employ an entirely local workforce, especially Johoreans. We do not want to recruit only production operators. Instead, we want to train technicians and skilled workers capable of operating robotic technology," he said.
Ravintharan said the company also plans to source plastic, metal and electronic components from local suppliers to ensure small and medium enterprises (SMEs) benefit from the project's supply chain.
He said Senai was selected for its strategic location within the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) and proximity to Senai International Airport, the North-South Expressway and the Port of Tanjung Pelepas.
Meanwhile, Indah Holding chairman Datuk Sivasuriyamoorthy Sundara Raja said the project supports the government's push for greater local participation in high-technology manufacturing and aligns with the New Industrial Master Plan 2030 (NIMP 2030) and the Johor Economic Transformation Plan.
Mida Johor director Mohamad Reduan Mohd Zabri said the project reflects growing participation by local companies in advanced manufacturing and supports efforts to develop Johor as a high-technology manufacturing hub.
"The project is also aligned with the Federal and Johor governments' goal of positioning Johor as a leading high-technology manufacturing hub," he said.
He said Mida would continue working with the Johor government and relevant agencies to identify more local companies with the potential to become part of the JS-SEZ ecosystem.
He added that growing investment in the JS-SEZ would create significant opportunities for local SMEs to participate in high-technology industries, supply chains and supporting sectors.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father recalls son's final wishes after fatal school bus tragedy
Article:
KLUANG: A 35-year-old father shared his profound grief after his 7-year-old son, Muhammad Umar, died after being hit by a school bus in Taman Sri Lalang yesterday afternoon.
Mohd Faaiz Muran said he was at work in Selangor when he was informed of the 4.30pm accident and rushed back immediately.
"I do not know how much pain he went through when I saw his body. I could not bear to look at his condition," Faaiz said when met after his son's funeral at the Sri Lalang Islamic Cemetery today.
He recalled that Umar had shown changes in his behaviour a few days before the incident, becoming unusually affectionate.
"Last Monday, he asked me to carry and hug him, which he had never done before. He also asked for various things, including a bicycle, and promised me that he would study hard.
"That is why I am so sad when I think about Umar. Fortunately, I managed to fulfil most of his requests. I was supposed to return to Taman Sri Kluang tomorrow and planned to take him out for a walk with his twin younger sisters," he said.
Despite the tragedy, Faaiz said the family does not blame the bus driver, accepting what happened as fate.
Earlier, Umar was buried at 2.15pm following a post-mortem examination at the Enche' Besar Hajjah Khalsom Hospital.
An atmosphere of deep sorrow enveloped the cemetery, where family members tried to comfort the boy's distraught mother, Nurul Shahira Salim, 35.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Discussions with other coalitions had my approval as PN chairman, Samsuri tells Muhyiddin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: All discussions and engagements with outside parties ahead of the Negri Sembilan election were conducted with the knowledge and permission of Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
In response to allegations made yesterday by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, Samsuri said these discussions have since been finalised and approved by the PN supreme council at today's meeting.
"With reference to the statement by the Bersatu president on July 15, please be informed that all initial discussion and engagement processes with any parties were carried out with my knowledge and permission as the chairman of Perikatan Nasional; and they have been finalised and approved by the PN supreme council at today's meeting," Samsuri said in a statement today.
Yesterday, Muhyiddin expressed disappointment with PN component party Pas for negotiating with Barisan Nasional without involving other parties in PN.
Muhyiddin claimed the move
lacked the approval
of the PN supreme council, describing Pas' actions as unilateral and contrary to the coalition's interests.
Samsuri said the coalition would announce its candidates today, noting that their participation is aimed at safeguarding public welfare, driving the state's development agenda, and preserving harmony within Negri Sembilan's multi-ethnic society.
He also said the PN supreme council had agreed to approve seat allocation negotiations for the Negri Sembilan assembly seats among its component parties, namely Pas, Wawasan, Gerakan, and MIPP.
PN candidates will also be permitted to use the "Perikatan Nasional" logo in the state polls, as previously agreed, Samsuri said.
This follows Muhyiddin's remarks yesterday that the Bersatu supreme leadership council had authorised him to permit candidates from other component parties to run under the Bersatu banner.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KWAN law closes loophole exposed by 2021 RM5bil withdrawal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The RM5 billion withdrawal from the Kumpulan Wang Amanah Negara (KWAN) in 2021 exposed gaps in the existing framework that the newly passed Bill seeks to address, with no future withdrawal allowed without a Dewan Rakyat resolution.
Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the 2021 episode, which drew public attention, highlighted three fundamental weaknesses in the 1988 National Trust Fund Act.
"Under the 1988 Act, such a large withdrawal could be approved by the minister without parliamentary approval.
"Under this Bill, that is no longer the case," he said while winding up debate on the second reading of the National Trust Fund Bill in the Dewan Rakyat.
The Bill was passed at both the second and third readings without amendments.
In 2021, then Finance Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz announced that the government would withdraw RM5 billion from KWAN to
finance
the National Covid-19 Immunisation Programme.
Liew said under the new Bill, any withdrawal exceeding the annual limit – capped at half of the fund's expected long-term real rate of return – would require a Dewan Rakyat resolution.
He added that the minister's directive powers would be limited to general directions that do not conflict with the Act.
On criminal accountability, Liew said board members, officers and staff of the new fund body would be considered public servants under the Penal Code, making them liable for criminal breach of trust under Section 409, which carries a maximum jail term of 20 years.
Failure to disclose a conflict of interest would be an offence punishable by a fine of up to RM250,000 or imprisonment of up to five years, while breaches of confidentiality could result in a fine of up to RM100,000 or imprisonment of up to three years. The MACC Act 2009 would also apply.
"This Bill provides a control architecture that did not exist at the time," Liew said, adding that concerns over ministerial powers over the fund had been raised in Parliament as early as 1987.
The legislation, comprising six parts and 44 clauses, replaces the 1988 Act and is built on four structural pillars.
One key change is the introduction of statutory contributions, which legally mandates inflows into the fund for the first time, ending four decades of reliance on Petronas as its sole voluntary contributor.
The Bill also limits withdrawals to three purposes – education, healthcare, and climate change mitigation and adaptation – while imposing a maximum annual withdrawal limit.
All withdrawals must be publicly disclosed, and recipients must report on fund utilisation until the money is fully spent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kota Damansara residents take objections over proposed data centre project online
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kota Damansara residents have raised objections to a proposed commercial data centre in their neighbourhood, citing concerns over traffic congestion, road safety, environmental impact and the project's proximity to residential areas.
The proposed development came to light through a public notice stating that the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ) had received applications for the surrender and re-alienation of land under Sections 197 and 76 under the National Land Code 1965, together with planning permission for a commercial data centre.
The notice invited adjoining landowners and members of the public to submit objections between July 9 and July 15.
Residents took to social media yesterday to voice their concerns, with many questioning the suitability of locating a data centre within a residential neighbourhood.
"Data centres don't belong in residential areas. There is plenty of available land elsewhere, especially in Rawang and even Sepang," user izhamznl wrote.
Meanwhile, user rajakamarul asked: "Will the residents live next to the data centre, which is known to emit high noise pollution, high electricity consumption, and high water usage?"
Others warned of the environmental impact of the proposed development.
"Data centres generate a lot of heat, mainly from their air-conditioning systems. The surrounding neighbourhood will inevitably become hotter, residents will have to run their air-conditioners longer, electricity consumption will rise, and temperatures in the area will increase further.
"In the end, it is the residents who will suffer the consequences," wrote user izdi.aizat.
User far.fella15, meanwhile, said: "The weather in Malaysia is like being in an oven now. Look at the data centre project in Hawaii and how bad the impact is on their environment."
Several residents also claimed they were unable to submit objections online because the contact details provided in the public notice were invalid, while others criticised the seven-day objection period as inadequate.
"The usual objection period is 14-30 days. Seven days is far too short. It is unfair to residents," wrote user safrizal82.
Some residents also urged Kota Damansara assemblyman Izuan Kasim to intervene.
Responding to the complaints, Izuan said his office would submit a formal objection alongside residents.
"An official objection will be made by my office together with the residents.
"Whether it is election season or not, we still have to act. I will bring all development projects opposed by residents or traders to MBPJ for consideration," he wrote in a Threads post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Germany's Delivery Hero agrees €12.7bil takeover by Uber
Article:
FRANKFURT: German food delivery group Delivery Hero said Thursday it has agreed to be taken over by US ride-hailing giant Uber in a €12.7 billion (US$14.6 billion) deal.
Founded in 2011, the German firm now operates in over 60 markets and is one of the world's biggest food delivery groups.
It has also expanded beyond its traditional food business to "quick commerce", delivering small packages to customers.
Uber is offering €41.50 per share for Delivery Hero, valuing the deal at €12.7 billion.
Delivery Hero's shares were down 0.5 per cent in Frankfurt after the announcement, trading at 37.9 euros.
"Uber's global mobility and delivery platform and our shared commitment to innovation make this the right partnership to build on Delivery Hero's strengths in local food delivery and quick commerce," said Niklas Oestberg, chief executive officer (CEO) and co-founder of Delivery Hero.
Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi said a merger would "extend affordable, reliable delivery to many millions more people in some of the world's most dynamic economies, while creating more opportunities for merchants and couriers".
Uber is acquiring Delivery Hero's businesses in 50 markets worldwide across Asia, Europe, Latin America and the Middle East.
A US investment firm, SSW Partners, is acquiring the German group's operations in another 14 markets, where Uber and Delivery Hero compete, for around €1.4 billion.
Delivery Hero said its management recommends that shareholders accept the deal, and it is expected to be finalised in the second half of 2027.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two debt collectors jailed 18 months, fined RM20k for harassing borrowers
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Two debt collectors were sentenced to 18 months' jail and fined RM20,000 each by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to harassing and threatening borrowers in March and April.
Khairul Helmy Abd Karim, 39, and Mohamad Irwan Abdul Halim, 43, entered the plea after the charges were read out to them before magistrate Noor Farah Hazwani Osman.
On the first charge, the duo were accused of acting with common intention to harass a man by splashing red paint on his car at Taman Sri Putra here at 3.30am on March 14.
On the second charge, they were accused of mutually harassing another man by splashing yellow paint on a Naza Forte car and the front gate of a house at Taman Berjaya here at 5.30am on April 21.
Both charges were framed under Section 29(B)(2) of the Moneylenders Act 1951, read together with Section 34 of the Penal Code. The offence carries a fine of between RM10,000 and RM50,000, a jail term of up to two years, or both, upon conviction.
For the first charge, Noor Farah Hazwani sentenced each accused to eight months' jail and a RM10,000 fine, in default of three months' imprisonment.
For the second charge, both men were fined RM10,000 and sentenced to 10 months' jail each. They face an additional four months' imprisonment if they fail to pay the fine.
Deputy public prosecutor Noor Hazwani Md Noor prosecuted, while both accused were represented by Nur Farahin Saad from the National Legal Aid Foundation (YBGK).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: 'Crab-walking' bus sparks road safety concerns
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A bus that appeared to move sideways "like a crab" while travelling forward has sparked concern among social media users over its roadworthiness.
A Threads user, @izmieunifi, shared a video of the bus and questioned how a vehicle in such a condition could still be allowed to operate on the road.
https://www.threads.com/@izmieunifi/post/Da0JoOglF_L?xmt=AQG0Iyne4uZ26e3enTEibnt_Dmkw8psPo_MnysCsskVqLp9-7kP0ulEeOnGN419F6dFJYOuS&source_surface=35&slof=1
The post drew numerous reactions from other Threads users, many of whom expressed concern over the bus' unusual movement.
User @nutrimablackg commented that the bus was moving "like a crab" and should no longer be allowed on the road.
Another user, @khuzairina67, questioned how the bus was still permitted to operate, adding that passengers should be concerned about their safety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Standing tall for 17 years, 'IPOH' sign ready for a makeover
Article:
IPOH: After standing tall for 17 years as one of the city's most recognisable landmarks, the iconic "IPOH" sign along Jalan Kuala Kangsar near Gunung Lang has been taken down for restoration works after showing signs of deterioration.
The towering landmark, which has welcomed countless visitors to the state capital since it was installed in 2009, is now set for a fresh new look following damage to the letter "H" during a storm last year.
The Ipoh City Council (MBI) in a statement said this was the first time the sign has been taken down since it was installed in 2009.
It added that the restoration works on the sign overlooking Gunung Lang began on Monday, with the refurbishment expected to be completed next month.
The council said the exercise was part of its continuous efforts to maintain city landmarks and public assets, ensuring they remain safe, well-maintained and visually appealing.
MBI added that the restoration also reflected its commitment to preserving Ipoh's image as a clean, attractive and liveable tourism destination.
"The works involve dismantling the existing lettering structure, followed by repair and refurbishment before the sign is reinstalled at the site.
"On July 13, a crane was brought to the location, while dismantling works were carried out from July 14 to 15.
"Repair and restoration works will take place from July 15 until Aug 3, followed by the reinstallation of the 'IPOH' sign from Aug 4 to 6," it said in a statement.
The council advised road users and residents around the Gunung Lang area to exercise caution and cooperate throughout the duration of the works to ensure they are carried out smoothly.
Meanwhile, Ipoh Mayor Datuk Zamakhshari Hanipah said the sign was temporarily removed to facilitate inspection, repair and restoration works before it is reinstalled early next month.
He said the maintenance work was necessary to ensure the structure remained safe, durable and well-preserved as an important city landmark.
"The council seeks the cooperation and patience of city residents throughout the implementation period," he said.
[[nid:1490249]]
Zamakhshari added that MBI remained committed to preserving public facilities and city landmarks for the safety, comfort and well-being of residents and visitors.
For many residents and visitors, the return of the iconic sign is eagerly anticipated as it holds sentimental value and has become closely associated with the experience of arriving in Ipoh.
Meru resident Ili Aqilah Yus Amirul, 36, said she had always looked forward to seeing the sign whenever she travelled along the highway.
She recalled noticing its deteriorating condition earlier this month and hoped the restoration could be completed soon.
"Actually, when I was driving along the highway in early July, I was looking for the sign and I remember thinking how bad the letter 'H' looked.
"I kept wondering when MBI would replace it because it was already in poor condition. The signage is very iconic," she said.
The senior communication officer, who currently works in Kuala Lumpur, said the landmark had become an important visual identity for Ipoh, especially for those entering the city from outside.
"For people coming from out of town like me, we always look forward to seeing the sign as an indication that we have arrived in Ipoh.
"Thousands of vehicles pass through that highway every day, so it is important that the first impression visitors get reflects the beauty of the city," she said.
[16/7/2026 3:35 PM] Leslie (NST): She added that the upgraded sign could attract more visitors, especially during school holidays.
"I just hope the new one can be installed quickly. When the school holidays come, there will definitely be more people wanting to see it," she said.
Meanwhile, Taman Seri Desa resident Ahmad Faizal Ramli, 42, said the sign had become part of his memories of the city, particularly whenever he returned home after travelling outstation.
"Whenever I see the 'IPOH' sign, it gives me the feeling that I am back home. It may look simple, but it carries a lot of meaning for locals.
"It is also something visitors remember because it represents the character of Ipoh," he said.
Faizal said the signage had also become a popular backdrop among visitors due to its location and scenic surroundings.
"The combination of the 'IPOH' sign, the limestone hills and the landscape makes it instantly recognisable.
"It has become part of Ipoh's visual identity and something people associate with the city," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Press freedom row: Media groups condemn alleged curbs at Ayob Khan event
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two media associations have condemned what they described as unnecessary restrictions imposed on journalists and photographers during an event attended by Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay today.
The Selangor Photographers and Journalists Association (Wajar) and the Crime Journalists Association (CiJA), in a joint statement, alleged that media photographers were barred from taking photographs and told to rely solely on official images provided by police.
They also claimed no press conference or impromptu interview with Ayob Khan was allowed after the event.
"Officers on the ground cited 'direct instructions from the Deputy IGP' as the reason for the restrictions, a claim that requires clarification," read the statement.
The associations alleged that some officers from the police Corporate Communications Unit had previously displayed conduct that hindered media personnel from carrying out their duties.
They said such actions undermined press freedom, deprived news organisations of independent and exclusive coverage, and weakened the long-standing relationship between the media and police.
Wajar and CiJA called on Bukit Aman and the Selangor police contingent headquarters to clarify whether the restrictions were imposed on Ayob's instructions.
They also urged police leadership to take appropriate action against any officer found to have acted outside established procedures or misused the names of senior police leaders to justify restrictions.
The associations called on police to reaffirm their commitment to respecting the professional role and independence of journalists and photographers.
"Police have long been a strategic partner of the media in informing the public. This relationship must continue to be built on mutual respect, transparency and professionalism, rather than unnecessary restrictions on legitimate journalistic work," read the statement.
The two associations said they remained committed to constructive engagement with police and hoped the matter would be resolved promptly to prevent similar incidents.
The New Straits Times has reached out to Ayob for clarification.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cultivating visionary policymakers in maritime education
Article:
LETTER:
The future of the maritime sector will not be determined solely by those who navigate ships, but by those who have the vision, knowledge, and courage to shape the policies that guide them.
Thus, maritime education must evolve beyond producing technically competent seafarers and marine professionals, given what has happened in maritime hotspots in the Middle East which is reshaping global geopolitics and geoeconomics.
In addition, as global shipping faces challenges such as decarbonisation, digitalisation, cybersecurity, autonomous vessels, and environmental protection, future maritime professionals require a broader understanding of how policies shape the sector.
The maritime domain is closely linked to national security, international trade, economic growth, environmental sustainability, and regional cooperation.
Technical expertise remains essential, but it must be complemented by policy knowledge to ensure sustainable, secure, and resilient maritime development.
Decisions by governments and international organisations directly influence shipping operations, port management and maritime safety.
Professionals working within this sector therefore need the ability to understand regulatory frameworks, assess policy implications, and contribute to evidence-based solutions.
Maritime education should cultivate these competencies from the earliest stages of learning.
Developing policy expertise begins with a curriculum that integrates governance, maritime law, public administration, strategic management and international regulations alongside traditional technical subjects.
Students should be encouraged to analyse complex maritime issues, evaluate competing interests, and appreciate the relationship between operational practices and public policy.
Research also plays a significant role in strengthening maritime policymaking. Research findings should not remain within academic institutions but should be translated into practical policy recommendations.
By doing so, higher education institutions can help governments and industry develop forward-looking, evidence-based decisions that promote sustainable maritime development.
Ultimately, excellence in maritime education depends not only on technical training but also on developing professionals who understand the importance of policymaking.
Strong partnerships between universities, government agencies and industry stakeholders are fundamental to ensure that curricula remain aligned with national priorities.
From ambition to action, maritime education must become the foundation for developing not only skilled professionals but also visionary policymakers.
DR IZYAN MUNIRAH MOHD ZAIDEEN
Senior lecturer
Faculty of Maritime Studies
Universiti Malaysia Terengganu
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lim Guan Eng urges Penang to defer water tariff increase
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Bagan Member of Parliament Lim Guan Eng has urged the Penang government to postpone a planned 20 sen per cubic metre water tariff increase for one year.
In a letter dated July 1 to Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, Lim appealed for the hike to be deferred, citing public concerns over rising living costs and economic pressures.
Lim said the proposed increase, from the current average tariff of 62 sen per cubic metre, would affect households and businesses already facing higher costs.
It was reported that more than 80 per cent of Penang households would pay more for water from this month following the revised tariffs.
State Infrastructure, Transport and Digital Committee chairman Zairil Khir Johari said households using 35 cubic metres of water a month would see an increase of up to RM2.55 under the new tariffs.
Lim argued that the additional RM20 million in annual revenue expected from the tariff increase would not significantly contribute towards the billions of ringgit required for water infrastructure development and upgrades.
He also pointed to Penang Water Supply Corporation's (PBAPP) improved financial performance, saying its profit before tax rose from RM23.1 million in the first quarter of 2025 to RM49.6 million in the corresponding period this year.
He said PBAPP's latest financial results indicated it could absorb the delay for another year without jeopardising its financial position.
"If PBAPP urgently needs the additional RM20 million, the state government can apply for a federal grant or loan. There is no reason for the federal government to refuse such assistance," he said.
Lim also said the state government should consider postponing the tariff revision to demonstrate its commitment to easing the cost of living burden on Penang residents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Padini says MACC has unfrozen all bank accounts linked to probe
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — Padini Holdings Berhad said today that the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has unfrozen all of the company’s and its subsidiaries’ bank accounts that had been frozen as part of an anti-money laundering investigation, adding that no one from the group has been arrested or charged.
In a public statement, the retailer said all affected accounts had been released after being frozen since April.
“The Company is pleased to announce that all affected bank accounts of the Company and the Group have been unfrozen by the MACC,” it said.
Padini also said no director, officer, employee or representative of the group had been arrested or charged in connection with the investigation.
It added that neither the company nor any member of the group had been made the subject of forfeiture proceedings arising from the case.
The company reiterated its long-held position that it had committed no wrongdoing.
“The Company wishes to emphasise that it has consistently maintained, from the outset of the investigation, that neither it nor any member of the Group was involved in any wrongdoing or improper conduct,” the statement said.
Padini said the group remained committed to “the highest standards of integrity, transparency, and corporate governance”, and maintained a strict zero-tolerance policy towards corruption, bribery and all forms of unlawful or unethical conduct.
The company’s bank accounts were frozen in April under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA) as part of an MACC investigation.
At the time, Padini said the affected accounts represented only a small portion of the group’s total deposits and that its retail operations continued without disruption.
In May, MACC said it had detained eight individuals and recorded statements from 10 others over alleged money laundering involving transactions linked to Padini.
The anti-graft agency also said the retailer itself was not a target of the investigation and was cooperating with investigators.
Padini thanked its shareholders, customers, business partners, employees and other stakeholders for their “continued trust, patience, and unwavering support throughout this period”.

Title: Perak unity govt remains stable despite political tensions elsewhere, says Saarani
Article:
IPOH, July 16 — Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today said the state’s unity government remains stable and will not be affected by political developments in other states that have led to tensions within governing coalitions.
Saarani stressed that Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders in Perak remain committed to serving the people and ensuring the state administration continues to function smoothly.
He said the situation in Perak differs from developments elsewhere, including in Melaka, where DAP recently announced its withdrawal from the state unity government.
“After DAP announced that it was leaving the unity government in Melaka, I sat down with my state executive council (exco) members before yesterday’s meeting and asked them frankly whether what had happened in Melaka would also happen in Perak.
“As of yesterday, the answer was no. We made a commitment that since we still have a long period left to serve the people, we will continue working together.
“What happens after the State Legislative Assembly is dissolved is another matter, but for now, until the assembly is dissolved, we will continue performing our duties as usual,” he said.
Saarani told reporters this when asked whether the growing differences between BN and PH leaders in other states would affect relations among members of the state government after launching the Halaman Suri Sejahtera programme in Taman Lapangan Terbang here.
“I do not quite agree with using the word ‘conflict’. Perhaps it is better described as differences in opinion and differences in approach.
“Some people speak loudly, while others speak softly. Some may have a loud voice but a kind heart. Everyone has their own way,” Saarani explained.
On Tuesday, four Melaka DAP state assemblymen announced their immediate withdrawal from the Melaka state government after the State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the state constitution allowing for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.
Yesterday, the DAP Central Executive Committee (CEC) backed the decision by its Melaka chapter to oppose the introduction of appointed assemblymen in the state.
Saarani said the commitment by BN and PH leaders in Perak to continue working together meant there was no issue affecting the administration of the state government despite political developments elsewhere.
Asked whether relations among leaders in the state unity government remained intact, Saarani said there was still no problem.
“This is politics. As Tun Dr Mahathir Mohamad once said, there are no permanent enemies and no permanent friends in politics.
“However, it is best that we do not hurt one another. Differences in opinion and differences in views are perfectly fine, but they should not lead to insults, condemnation, vulgar language or breaking relationships.
“We are all human beings. We should be careful not to speak carelessly in a way that hurts or offends other people’s feelings,” he said.

Title: Penang State Museum to reopen on July 23 after nearly a decade of restoration
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, July 16 — The Penang State Museum on Farquhar Street, which has been closed for restoration works since 2017, will finally reopen next week.
State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said restoration works on the heritage building have finally been completed.
“We will be officially opening the newly upgraded museum on July 23,” he said in his speech at the launch of the Traditional Trades Campaign 2026 here today.
He said July is an appropriate month to reopen the museum as it is also when the state’s heritage celebrations are held, with Heritage Day falling on July 7.
“Two days after that, on July 25, we will also be officially reopening Fort Cornwallis after restoration works,” he added.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and guests from the Aga Khan Trust for Culture are expected to attend the reopening of Fort Cornwallis following the restoration of its moat.
The moat surrounding the fort was originally built in 1804 to protect it during the Napoleonic Wars.
It was filled in in 1921 due to a malaria outbreak, which led to poor drainage and occasional flooding in nearby areas.
Restoration works at Fort Cornwallis, including the reconstruction of the moat, began around 2022.
Water once again fills the restored moat at Fort Cornwallis after conservation works revived a feature that had been filled in more than a century ago. — Picture by Opalyn Mok
The restored moat forms a 4,000-square-metre water basin measuring 0.9 metres deep and capable of holding about 3,600 cubic metres of water.
As for the Penang State Museum, it closed in 2017 for RM18 million in restoration works, with the second phase involving interior restoration beginning in 2024.
The restoration works were suspended during the Covid-19 pandemic from 2020 to 2023.
Last year, the Penang Public Works Department (JKR) issued a notice of non-compliance to the contractor over delays in the project.
A new contractor was subsequently appointed to complete the remaining works, and JKR handed the museum building over to the Penang State Museum Department on March 9 this year.
The Penang State Museum building has a history spanning about 200 years.
The first phase of the building was constructed in 1896, while the second phase was completed in 1906.
It housed Penang Free School until 1927 before being occupied by Hutchings School until its old wing was destroyed during the Second World War.
The building was repurposed to house the state museum in 1965.

Title: Govt weighs SST relief for elderly care centres amid cost concerns
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 16 — The Ministry of Finance (MoF), together with the Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development (KPWKM), is conducting a further study on a proposal to exempt elderly care centres from the Sales and Service Tax (SST).
Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the study includes a review of the existing service tax to identify categories of care centres providing basic or premium services.
He said the move was important to ensure that the targeted eight per cent service tax would not adversely affect or burden vulnerable groups already facing a high cost of living.
“Any proposals for improvements from all stakeholders will also be taken into consideration before the study is finalised,” he said during the Dewan Rakyat Special Chamber session today.
He was responding to Lee Chuan How (PH-Ipoh Timor), who urged the government to exempt Malaysians from the eight per cent SST on elderly care services provided by care centres registered with the Social Welfare Department (JKM).
In raising the matter, Lee said the SST had placed an additional financial burden on ordinary families, as monthly fees of around RM2,500 meant they had to bear significantly higher costs.
